text:
**Key projects:**
*   Marine MRV (measurement, reporting, and verification) system to finance the protection of marine habitats [cite: 61].
*   Nature-based currencies [cite: 61].
*   Independent Climate Accountability Network for the Global Stocktake [cite: 63].
**Collaboration potential:**
Very High.
They possess the exact technical infrastructure (spatial mesh, active inference, planetary digital twins) needed to power the computational backend of a planetary simulation.
**Governance model:**
Operates as a 501(c)(3) umbrella institution containing a lab, software division, endowment, and network, heavily emphasizing systems thinking and radical collaboration [cite: 63, 65].
**Community size:**
Maintains a small, highly specialized team of 10 or fewer core employees, working with a massive global network of collaborators [cite: 68].
---

## Planetary Guardians / SpiralWeb

**Name:** Planetary Guardians / SpiralWeb
**Type:** NGO / Protocol
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://www.planetaryguardians.org (and https://spiralweb.earth)
**Location:** Global (Denmark / Uganda / Morocco / Pakistan mentioned in internal notes)
**Scale:** Emerging
**Maturity:** Emerging

**What they do:**
Planetary Guardians is a collective of global leaders that promotes the "Planetary Boundaries" scientific framework to guide Earth system protection [cite: 69, 70].
SpiralWeb acts as the applied technological counterpart: an Ostrom-based, landscape-scale regenerative governance protocol and coordination infrastructure for land stewards [cite: 71].
Together, they aim to build tools to measure, verify, and govern the "Planetary Commons" [cite: 70, 71].
**Key people:**
*   Lars A.
Engberg, Independent Scholar / Architect of SpiralWeb [cite: 71]
*   Johan Rockström, Director of the Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research (PIK) [cite: 69, 70, 72]
*   Dr.
Ralph Chami, Financial Economist [cite: 69]
*   Robert Redford, Planetary Guardian Emeritus [cite: 69]
*   Mary Robinson, Facilitator (Global Ethical Stocktake) [cite: 72]
*   Xiye Bastida, Hindou Oumarou Ibrahim, Christiana Figueres, Paul Polman, Dr.
Sylvia Earle (Active Guardians) [cite: 72]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research (PIK) [cite: 70, 72]
*   Global Commons Alliance Earth HQ [cite: 70]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   Planetary Boundaries Science Lab events / Global summits [cite: 72]

**Publications, reports, or open-source tools:**
*   *Planetary Health Check* (A comprehensive scientific report and evolving digital tool monitoring Earth's vital organs, launched Sept 2024/2025) [cite: 70, 73].
*   *SRIP 1.0 protocol file* (SpiralWeb protocol architecture) [cite: 71].
*   20 working papers on Moral Biology and Planetary Guardianship [cite: 71].
*   *Penguin Dashboard* (Transparency instrument) [cite: 71].
**Key projects:**
*   Planetary Boundaries Analyzer (in development by Hannes Rauhe at PIK) [cite: 72]
*   SpiralWeb Field Nodes (pilot tracking of steward capacity and land interventions) [cite: 71]

**Governance model:**
SpiralWeb operates under the "Spiralweb Stewardship Association" using a three-stream public support architecture: Land Stewardship Support, Cultural and Knowledge Support, and Coordination and Partner Infrastructure [cite: 71].
It is heavily evidence-bound, scaling integration based on verifiable field data [cite: 71].
---

## ÆRTH

**Name:** ÆRTH
**Type:** Company / Non-Profit Initiative
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://www.aerth.live
**Location:** Global
**Scale:** Small
**Maturity:** Emerging
**Flags:** Direct Collaborator Potential (High)

**What they do:**
ÆRTH is a decentralized environmental data initiative creating a real-time, dynamic "digital twin" (Planetary AI) of the Earth [cite: 74, 75].
By unifying on-the-ground ecological data with satellite observation streams and applying machine learning, ÆRTH identifies complex ecosystem feedback loops (e.g., Saharan sand fertilizing the Amazon) to predict ecological impacts and simulate regeneration strategies [cite: 75, 76, 77].
**Key people:**
*   Adina Popescu, Founder, CEO, Philosopher, and Technologist [cite: 74, 75, 78, 79]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Earth Observation scientists from NASA JPL [cite: 76]
*   Global Ocean Science Institutions [cite: 76]
*   Conservation International (Adina previously advised) [cite: 75]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   Funding the Commons SF 2025 (Panel on AI & Emerging Technologies for Ecological Resilience) [cite: 79]
*   Sustainability Now Podcast, Cacao Conversations Podcast [cite: 74, 75]

**Publications, reports, or open-source tools:**
*   **Open-source tools:** Developing an open-source, distributed environmental data set and unifying science network [cite: 76].
**Key projects:**
*   Global Environmental Science Data & Standards Cooperative [cite: 76]
*   Planetary AI digital twin platform [cite: 80]

**Collaboration potential:**
High. ÆRTH is literally building a computational "Planetary AI" to simulate global ecology—the perfect digital foundation for an Earth-scale governance simulation.
---

## Regen Network

**Name:** Regen Network
**Type:** Company / Blockchain Platform
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://www.regen.network
**Location:** Global
**Scale:** Medium
**Maturity:** Mature

**What they do:**
Regen Network is an ecological operating system leveraging blockchain technology to track, verify, and reward regenerative land management [cite: 81, 82, 83].
Using the Cosmos SDK, they built a public Proof-of-Stake ledger where farmers and land stewards can issue scientifically verified digital assets (like CarbonPlus credits) representing carbon sequestration and biodiversity gains, which are then sold on transparent markets [cite: 84, 85].
**Key people:**
*   Gregory Landua, CEO and Co-Founder [cite: 82, 85, 86]
*   Christian Shearer, CIO and Co-Founder [cite: 82, 85, 87]
*   Sara Johnstone, COO [cite: 85]
*   Aaron Craelius, CTO [cite: 85]
*   Ron Steinherz, VP of Product [cite: 85]
*   Sarah Baxendell, Director of Finance [cite: 85]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Terrasos [cite: 88]
*   ecoToken [cite: 88]
*   Solana Foundation [cite: 88]
*   Buckminster Fuller Institute [cite: 82]
*   NORI [cite: 82]
*   FarmOS [cite: 82]
*   Terra Genesis International [cite: 82]
*   GOLEM [cite: 83]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   Funding the Commons SF 2025 [cite: 79]
*   Devcon 3 (Cancun, historic) [cite: 83]

**Publications, reports, or open-source tools:**
*   **Open-source tools:** Regen Ledger, `x/ecocredit`, Regen Registry, data anchoring modules, `agentic-tokenomics` [cite: 85, 89].
